description:
## Background
The staged no-useEffect rule prevents new direct calls but does not yet make the repository policy enforceable. Once all existing call sites have been migrated, the rule can become a blocking invariant.

## What to build
Promote the no-useEffect Oxlint rule from warning to error after all six migration tasks are complete. Verify that the repository contains no direct `useEffect` calls outside the narrowly scoped `useMountEffect` implementation, if that escape hatch exists.

## Technical Approach
Remove any temporary migration-only configuration and change the rule severity to blocking. Keep only the intentional wrapper suppression required by `useMountEffect`; do not preserve file-level or call-site exceptions. Run the complete relevant lint, typecheck, unit, and build verification.

## Considerations
This task must remain blocked by every effect migration so its own change lands on a clean tree. The final rule should continue to provide a replacement-oriented diagnostic, and the enforcement check must not prohibit the documented escape hatch itself.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All six catalogued direct `useEffect` call sites have been removed or replaced.
- [ ] The no-useEffect rule is configured at error severity.
- [ ] No migration-only allowlists or call-site suppressions remain.
- [ ] The intentional `useMountEffect` wrapper, if present, is the only narrowly scoped suppression.
- [ ] Lint, typecheck, tests, and `pnpm build` pass with the blocking rule enabled.

## Test Plan
- Run a repository-wide search for direct `useEffect` calls.
- Run the complete Ultracite/Oxlint check.
- Run TypeScript, unit, and relevant desktop/E2E verification.
- Run `pnpm build`.

Triage: ready-for-agent.
title: Promote no-useEffect Oxlint rule to blocking enforcement
